item: omnia-conformance: map W1-mask / sentinel-collision (option+result predicates refuted)
reachability_fact: map.iii:56 `MAP_IDX_FULL = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFu64` has bit 63 set (= MAP_FOUND_BIT). `map_probe_internal` returns MAP_IDX_FULL when every slot is occupied (100% fill, no tombstones). Three read-side consumers check `(r & MAP_FOUND_BIT)` to distinguish found vs. absent: `map_u32u32_get` line 370, `map_u32u32_contains` line 380, `map_u32u32_remove` line 419. Because MAP_IDX_FULL has bit 63 set, all three misclassify the "table full, key absent" probe result as found. `map_u32u32_get` and `map_u32u32_remove` then extract `idx = MAP_IDX_FULL & 0xFFFFFFFFu64 = 0xFFFFFFFF` and call `map_load_u32_le(MAP_VALUES_BASE[s], 0xFFFFFFFF)` — an out-of-bounds read ~17 GB past the buffer base. `map_u32u32_contains` returns a false-positive 1u8 for a key that is not present. `map_u32u32_put` is the one safe consumer: it checks `if r == MAP_IDX_FULL` at line 399 before any idx extraction. The mask `& 0xFFFFFFFFu64` at get:371, remove:420, put:402 is also wrong: if hard_max > 2^32 (legal per the u64 parameter), probe indices above 2^32-1 get silently truncated to the wrong slot. The reachability condition: call `map_u32u32_new(arena, N)` with `hard_max == N` so `map_grow` is blocked (line 246: `new_cap == cap -> MAP_E_FULL`), insert exactly N distinct keys with no removes (zero tombstones, 100% OCCUPIED), then call get/contains/remove on a key not in the table. On the broken code: contains returns 1 (false positive), get and remove dereference base+17GB. Callers that exercise this: corpus/21_map_put_get_grow_integrity.iii line 58 tests `contains(999u32)` for 0u8 on a map that is NOT at 100% fill so the bug does not fire there; the bug is unreachable from corpus/21 but reachable from any caller that fills to hard_max with no deletions. option/result predicates are REFUTED: option.iii line 31 encodes `some(v) = (v<<1)|1` (odd), none=0 (even); `is_some` line 32 checks `(o&1)==1`, `is_none` line 33 checks `(o&1)==0` — exact complement, verified in omnia_option.iii.o.s lines 621/1019 (andq then cmpq 1 vs 0). result.iii line 48 encodes `ok(v)=(v<<1)|RES_TAG_OK` where RES_TAG_OK=0 (even); err=(code<<1)|1 (odd); `is_ok` line 50 checks `(r&1)==0`, `is_err` line 51 checks `(r&1)==1` — correct. u64 side-table predicates guard via `s >= OPTU64_SLOTS` / `RES_LIVE[si]` checks. All option/result encode-predicate pairs are conformant.
maximal_design: Introduce a dedicated MAP_FULL_BIT (bit 62) as the "no slot available" signal in map_probe_internal's return value, completely disjoint from MAP_FOUND_BIT (bit 63). This retires MAP_IDX_FULL and eliminates the sentinel-collision. Simultaneously replace the 32-bit idx extraction mask (0xFFFFFFFFu64) with MAP_IDX_MASK (0x3FFFFFFFFFFFFFFFu64 = bits 0-61), covering all cap values that can realistically be allocated (arena_alloc1(cap*4) makes cap near 2^32 unallocatable in practice, so bits 62-63 are structurally free; the mask encodes this invariant explicitly rather than implicitly). The redesign makes the four consumers consistent without behavioral modification: get/contains/remove's FOUND-check (r & MAP_FOUND_BIT) is automatically correct because MAP_FULL_BIT does NOT set bit 63 -- a full-table probe now falls through to the bit-63-clear branch and returns none/0/none correctly. Only put's equality guard changes (== MAP_IDX_FULL to bit-62 test). result: every probe outcome is distinguishable: found=(bit63 set, bit62 clear, idx in bits 0-61), full=(bit63 clear, bit62 set), insert-slot=(bit63 clear, bit62 clear, idx in bits 0-61). No public ABI changes -- map_probe_internal is not @export; all public fn signatures are identical; set.iii uses only public fns and requires no changes.

code_changes.anchor:
const MAP_FOUND_BIT : u64 = 0x8000000000000000u64
const MAP_IDX_FULL  : u64 = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFu64
code_changes.new_code:
/* Probe-result encoding (map_probe_internal return value):
 *   bit 63 = MAP_FOUND_BIT : key was found; idx in bits 0..61 is the slot.
 *   bit 62 = MAP_FULL_BIT  : table is 100% occupied, no slot available.
 *   neither bit set        : key absent, insert into slot at bits 0..61.
 * MAP_IDX_MASK extracts the slot index without aliasing the flag bits.    */
const MAP_FOUND_BIT : u64 = 0x8000000000000000u64
const MAP_FULL_BIT  : u64 = 0x4000000000000000u64
const MAP_IDX_MASK  : u64 = 0x3FFFFFFFFFFFFFFFu64

kat:
Corpus test: STDLIB/corpus/772_map_full_table_sentinel.iii

Positive arm (non-full table, absent key): new map with 3/8 slots filled; get/contains/remove on key 999 must all return none/0/none. Verifies the non-full early-exit path does not regress.

Negative arm (full table, absent key -- the FALSIFIER): new map with hard_max=8 (initial_cap=8, grow blocked). Insert all 8 distinct keys (0..7) with no removes -> 100% OCCUPIED, zero tombstones. Probe missing key 999:
  - contains(999) must return 0u8. BROKEN: map_probe_internal returns 0xFFFFFFFF...FFFF (MAP_IDX_FULL), bit 63 set aliases MAP_FOUND_BIT, contains returns 1u8 (false positive) -> exit 40 signals the bug without crashing.
  - get(999) must return 0u64 (none). BROKEN: same sentinel -> extracts idx=0xFFFFFFFF -> map_load_u32_le at base+17GB -> segfault.
  - remove(999) must return 0u64 (none) and len must stay 8. BROKEN: same OOB + tombstones a bogus slot + decrements len.
  FIXED: probe returns MAP_FULL_BIT (bit 62, disjoint from bit 63), all three consumers see bit 63 clear, return none/0/none correctly -> exit 99.
